name: Trp Operon (Attenuation)
description: Trp operon control: high tryptophan lets the Trp repressor block the operator, while at low tryptophan transcription proceeds and attenuation via leader-peptide ribosome stalling decides whether RNA polymerase reads through to the structural genes.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Tryptophan level"]
    N2{"Trp high?"}
    N3["Repressor binds operator"]
    N4["Transcription blocked"]
    N5["Pol transcribes leader"]
    N6{"Ribosome stalls at Trp codons?"}
    N7["Antiterminator (2-3) forms"]
    N8["Read through to genes"]
    N9["Terminator (3-4) forms"]
    N10["Attenuation: stops"]
    N11["Trp biosynthesis enzymes"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|high| N3
    N3 --> N4
    N2 -->|low| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|stall| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N11
    N6 -->|no stall| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

nodeDetails.detail: Low Trp: transcription of the leader region

sources.title: Attenuation in the control of expression of bacterial operons
Transcription attenuation: once viewed as a novel regulatory strategy, it has been found in many forms
Transcription attenuation: a highly conserved regulatory strategy used by bacteria
